raw_consumer_statement:
To Whom It May Concern, I am responding to Citibanks continued position that the merchant provided evidence that I was given the option to view or download the sewer camera inspection video.

If such evidence exists, I am formally requesting that it be provided to me in verifiable form. 

Specifically, please provide : The actual sewer camera inspection video, including date and time stamp The name of the application the merchant claims I was instructed to download Timestamped proof showing when and how I was provided access to the video Any documentation, communication, or system record confirming that video access was delivered to me To date, none of this has been provided. 

Citibank continues to rely on a general statement that evidence was provided without producing that evidence. A signed invoice does not verify delivery of a separate digital product such as video footage. 

If the inspection video exists as claimed, it should be easily retrievable with a date and time stamp. The inability to produce this raises serious concerns as to whether the service was fully delivered. 

This matter remains unresolved solely because the video I paid for has not been provided. Producing the video would immediately resolve this dispute. 

I am requesting that Citibank either : XXXX. Provide the timestamped evidence referenced in your decision, or XXXX. Reconsider the dispute due to lack of verifiable proof of delivery Please provide a written response.
